title: New Devices Can't Get an IP in the BYOD Conference VLAN

ticket.summary: Attendees in the 3rd floor conference center (BYOD-VLAN, VLAN 50) report their laptops and phones show 'Limited connectivity' / 'No internet' and never get an IP address. Staff devices already connected earlier this morning are fine. Issue started around 10:30 AM when a 200-person all-hands meeting began.

topology.description: The conference center BYOD network (VLAN 50, 10.50.4.0/24) is served by a central DHCP server dhcp-01 (10.1.1.5) via IP helper on the floor's L3 switch sw-3f-core. The scope for VLAN 50 was originally sized for a small huddle room and has not been resized since the conference center was expanded to seat 200 people.

logs.content:
10:31:02 DHCPDISCOVER from 3c:22:fb:11:aa:02 via 10.50.4.1: no free leases in scope 10.50.4.0/24
10:31:02 DHCPNAK to 3c:22:fb:11:aa:02
10:31:19 DHCPDISCOVER from a4:5e:60:cc:19:44 via 10.50.4.1: no free leases in scope 10.50.4.0/24
10:31:19 DHCPNAK to a4:5e:60:cc:19:44
10:31:45 DHCPDISCOVER from 88:e9:fe:02:7d:10 via 10.50.4.1: no free leases in scope 10.50.4.0/24
10:31:45 DHCPNAK to 88:e9:fe:02:7d:10

cli_outputs.output:
Scope: 10.50.4.0/24 (BYOD-VLAN-50)
Address range: 10.50.4.10 - 10.50.4.30 (21 usable addresses)
Addresses leased: 21/21 (100%)
Addresses available: 0
Default lease time: 24 hours
Pending offers: 14

cli_outputs.command: show ip dhcp relay statistics vlan 50
cli_outputs.output:
VLAN 50 relay agent (ip helper-address 10.1.1.5)
DHCPDISCOVER forwarded: 187
DHCPOFFER relayed to clients: 21
DHCPNAK relayed to clients: 166

monitoring.notes: Dashboard shows scope 10.50.4.0/24 utilization sitting at a steady 60-70% (12-14 of 21 addresses) for the past month, then spiking to 100% at 10:29 AM today, right as the all-hands meeting began, and staying pinned at 100% since. The relay counters on sw-3f-core confirm DHCPDISCOVER traffic is reaching dhcp-01 fine — it's the pool itself that's out of addresses.

hints: Confirm the DHCP relay/helper path is actually working before assuming it's a relay problem — check whether discovers are reaching the DHCP server at all.
Look at the scope statistics on dhcp-01 for VLAN 50 specifically, not just overall server health.
The scope 10.50.4.0/24 only has 21 usable addresses (10.50.4.10-10.50.4.30) and all 21 are leased — the pool was sized for a small huddle room, not a 200-person conference.

solution.root_cause: The DHCP scope for VLAN 50 (10.50.4.10-10.50.4.30, only 21 usable addresses) is fully exhausted because it was sized for a small huddle room and never resized after the conference center was expanded to seat 200 people. New DHCPDISCOVER requests are correctly relayed to dhcp-01 but receive DHCPNAK since no free leases remain.
solution.fix: Expand the scope range for VLAN 50 (e.g. to a /23 or larger contiguous range) and/or shorten the lease time for that VLAN to free up addresses faster between meetings, then verify utilization drops back below capacity.
